meta.title: Sati translator-drift: memory→mindfulness at the English-translator boundary

results.label: 00_sanity_regex_nonzero
results.sql: -- FROZEN-REGEX SANITY: every Pali lexicon must return >0 on is_primary canon, else a diacritic is wrong and the study silently zeroes. SELECT 'sati_stem' AS lex, count(*) FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* '\msati|satipaṭṭhān|satipaṭṭhan|satupaṭṭhān|\msatim|\msatiṁ|\msatiṃ|\mpatissat|\mpaṭissat|sammāsati|satindriy|satisambojjh' UNION ALL SELECT 'native_memory', count(*) FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* '\msarat|\manussar|\manussat|\mpaṭissar|\msaraṇ|\mdhāraṇ|\mmuṭṭhassat|\masammos|\masammuss|apilāpan|appilāpan' UNION ALL SELECT 'negctrl_bhikkhu', count(*) FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* '\mbhikkhave\b|\mbhikkhu(?![a-zāīūṅñṭḍṇḷ])|\mbhikkhū';

results.label: 01_sati_stem_density_per_stratum_per_Mchar
results.sql: -- PRIMARY scoping density (descriptive context, NOT the drift test): sati-stem char-weighted density by stratum. Per-Mchar via per-layer denominators. WITH hits AS ( SELECT stratum(work_slug) AS strat, work_role, count(*) AS n_pass, sum(char_length(original)) AS hit_chars FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* '\msati|satipaṭṭhān|satipaṭṭhan|satupaṭṭhān|\msatim|\msatiṁ|\msatiṃ|\mpatissat|\mpaṭissat|\mpatissat|sammāsati|satindriy|satibal|satisambojjh|upaṭṭhitasat' GROUP BY 1,2 ) SELECT strat, work_role, n_pass, hit_chars, round( n_pass / CASE work_role WHEN 'mula' THEN 53.543921 WHEN 'attha' THEN 29.488533 WHEN 'tika' THEN 28.357746 END, 4) AS pass_per_Mchar FROM hits ORDER BY work_role, strat;

results.label: 02_native_memory_lexeme_density_DESCRIPTIVE
results.sql: -- DEMOTED descriptive supplement: native memory-vocab density per stratum per-Mchar. NOT used as convergent evidence. saṁsāra/saraṇa-refuge excluded by post-filter note. WITH hits AS ( SELECT stratum(work_slug) AS strat, work_role, count(*) AS n_pass FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* '\msarat|\msaranti|\manussar|\manussat|\mpaṭissar|\mdhāraṇ|\mmuṭṭhassat|\mmuṭṭhasat|\masammos|\masammuss|\masammuṭṭh|apilāpan|appilāpan' AND original !~* 'saṁsār|saṃsār' GROUP BY 1,2 ) SELECT strat, work_role, n_pass, round( n_pass / CASE work_role WHEN 'mula' THEN 53.543921 WHEN 'attha' THEN 29.488533 WHEN 'tika' THEN 28.357746 END, 4) AS pass_per_Mchar FROM hits ORDER BY work_role, strat;

results.label: 03_translator_coverage_on_sati_passages
results.sql: -- Which translators actually cover sati-stem passages, and how many — establishes per-translator denominators BEFORE any gloss classification. SELECT t.translator, count(DISTINCT p.id) AS sati_passages, sum(char_length(t.text)) AS en_chars FROM passages p JOIN translations t ON t.passage_id = p.id WHERE p.is_primary AND p.original ~* '\msati|satipaṭṭhān|satipaṭṭhan|satupaṭṭhān|\msatim|\msatiṁ|\msatiṃ|sammāsati|satindriy|satisambojjh|\mpaṭissat|\mpatissat' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY sati_passages DESC;

results.label: 04_gloss_share_by_translator_DRIFT_PRIMARY
results.sql: -- PRIMARY DRIFT PASS: deterministic gloss-class share per translator over sati-stem passages. Mutually-exclusive priority handled in the CASE (mindfulness > memory > awareness > other only for the deterministic pre-pass; ties + multi-class rows are RE-flagged for audit/embedding tie-break by col multi_flag). WITH base AS ( SELECT t.translator, p.id AS pid, t.text AS en, (t.text ~* '(?i)\mmindful')::int AS m_mind, (t.text ~* '(?i)\mmemor|\mremember|\mremembr|\mrecollect|\mrecall|\mretenti|bear(s|ing)? in mind|call(s|ing)? to mind|keep(s|ing)? in mind|not forget|unforget')::int AS m_mem, (t.text ~* '(?i)\maware|\mattenti|\mattend(s|ing)?|\mheedful|\mvigilan|\malert|presence of mind|\mwatchful')::int AS m_awar FROM passages p JOIN translations t ON t.passage_id = p.id WHERE p.is_primary AND p.original ~* '\msati|satipaṭṭhān|satipaṭṭhan|satupaṭṭhān|\msatim|\msatiṁ|\msatiṃ|sammāsati|satindriy|satisambojjh|\mpaṭissat|\mpatissat' ), cls AS ( SELECT translator, pid, CASE WHEN m_mind=1 THEN 'mindfulness' WHEN m_mem=1 THEN 'memory' WHEN m_awar=1 THEN 'awareness' ELSE 'other' END AS gloss, (m_mind+m_mem+m_awar) AS n_classes FROM base ) SELECT translator, gloss, count(*) AS n, round(100.0*count(*)/sum(count(*)) OVER (PARTITION BY translator),2) AS pct, sum((n_classes>1)::int) AS multi_flag FROM cls GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY translator, gloss;

results.label: 05_gloss_share_by_translator_AND_stratum
results.sql: -- DRIFT vs SOURCE-STRATUM decomposition: is the variance across translators (H1) or across strata? Same classification, grouped by translator x stratum. (T1: stratum is a lookup, present as source-layer contrast only.) WITH base AS ( SELECT t.translator, stratum(p.work_slug) AS strat, t.text AS en, (t.text ~* '(?i)\mmindful')::int AS m_mind, (t.text ~* '(?i)\mmemor|\mremember|\mrecollect|\mrecall|bear(s|ing)? in mind|call(s|ing)? to mind|not forget|unforget')::int AS m_mem, (t.text ~* '(?i)\maware|\mattenti|\mheedful|\mvigilan|\malert|presence of mind')::int AS m_awar FROM passages p JOIN translations t ON t.passage_id = p.id WHERE p.is_primary AND p.original ~* '\msati|satipaṭṭhān|satipaṭṭhan|sammāsati|satindriy|satisambojjh|\mpaṭissat|\mpatissat' ) SELECT translator, strat, CASE WHEN m_mind=1 THEN 'mindfulness' WHEN m_mem=1 THEN 'memory' WHEN m_awar=1 THEN 'awareness' ELSE 'other' END AS gloss, count(*) AS n FROM base GROUP BY 1,2,3 ORDER BY translator, strat, gloss;

results.label: 06_collocation_anussati_recollection_context
results.sql: -- CO-OCCURRENCE pass: where the Pali itself is in an explicit recollection compound (anussati / -anussati buddhanussati etc.), do translators STILL pick 'memory/recollection'? Isolates the strongest drift test — Pali forces recollection, English may not follow. SELECT t.translator, count(*) FILTER (WHERE t.text ~* '(?i)\mrecollect|\mmemor|\mremember|\mrecall|call(s|ing)? to mind') AS recollection_en, count(*) FILTER (WHERE t.text ~* '(?i)\mmindful') AS mindfulness_en, count(*) AS total FROM passages p JOIN translations t ON t.passage_id = p.id WHERE p.is_primary AND p.original ~* '\manussati|\manussar|buddhānussat|dhammānussat|saṅghānussat|\mcha anussat|cha-anussat' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY total DESC;

results.label: 07_rows_to_code_BLIND_AUDIT_SAMPLE
results.sql: -- ROWS TO BE HAND/AGENT-CODED for κ. Stratified blind sample: per translator, draw deterministic rows across all four deterministic classes incl 'other' and multi-class ties. Emits pid+translator+Pali+EN with NO pre-assigned label (audit is blind). Cap ~25/translator -> ~150-200 rows. WITH base AS ( SELECT t.translator, p.id AS pid, p.work_slug, p.original AS pali, t.text AS en, (t.text ~* '(?i)\mmindful')::int AS m_mind, (t.text ~* '(?i)\mmemor|\mremember|\mrecollect|\mrecall|bear(s|ing)? in mind|not forget')::int AS m_mem, (t.text ~* '(?i)\maware|\mattenti|\mheedful|\mvigilan|\malert')::int AS m_awar, md5(p.id::text || t.translator) AS shuf FROM passages p JOIN translations t ON t.passage_id = p.id WHERE p.is_primary AND p.original ~* '\msati|satipaṭṭhān|sammāsati|satindriy|satisambojjh|\manussat|\mpaṭissat' ), tagged AS ( SELECT *, (m_mind+m_mem+m_awar) AS nclass, CASE WHEN m_mind+m_mem+m_awar>1 THEN 'TIE' WHEN m_mind=1 THEN 'mind' WHEN m_mem=1 THEN 'mem' WHEN m_awar=1 THEN 'awar' ELSE 'other' END AS detcls, row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY translator, (CASE WHEN m_mind+m_mem+m_awar>1 THEN 'TIE' WHEN m_mind=1 THEN 'mind' WHEN m_mem=1 THEN 'mem' WHEN m_awar=1 THEN 'awar' ELSE 'other' END) ORDER BY md5(pid::text||translator)) AS rn FROM base ) SELECT translator, detcls, pid, work_slug, left(pali,400) AS pali_excerpt, left(en,400) AS en_excerpt FROM tagged WHERE rn <= 8 ORDER BY translator, detcls, rn;

results.label: 08_negative_control_glosses
results.sql: -- NEGATIVE CONTROL: on matched non-sati passages (bhikkhu-stem, same suttas/register), do translators diverge in rendering? Expect each translator ~one stable token (monk/mendicant/bhikkhu). Divergence here = generic idiolect, not sati drift. SELECT t.translator, count(*) FILTER (WHERE t.text ~* '(?i)\mmonk') AS monk, count(*) FILTER (WHERE t.text ~* '(?i)\mmendicant') AS mendicant, count(*) FILTER (WHERE t.text ~* '(?i)\mbhikkhu') AS bhikkhu_kept, count(*) AS total FROM passages p JOIN translations t ON t.passage_id = p.id WHERE p.is_primary AND p.original ~* '\mbhikkhave\b|\mbhikkhu(?![a-zāīūṅñṭḍṇḷ])|\mbhikkhū|\mbhikkhuno' AND p.original !~* '\msati|satipaṭṭhān|sammāsati|satindriy' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY total DESC;

results.label: 09_negctrl_within_translator_entropy
results.sql: -- NEGATIVE-CONTROL quantifier: per-translator rendering ENTROPY for the control stem (low = stable single token) to contrast against sati gloss entropy from 04. If control entropy ≈ sati entropy, drift is artifactual. WITH g AS ( SELECT t.translator, CASE WHEN t.text ~* '(?i)\mmendicant' THEN 'mendicant' WHEN t.text ~* '(?i)\mmonk' THEN 'monk' WHEN t.text ~* '(?i)\mbhikkhu' THEN 'bhikkhu' ELSE 'other' END AS tok FROM passages p JOIN translations t ON t.passage_id=p.id WHERE p.is_primary AND p.original ~* '\mbhikkhave\b|\mbhikkhu(?![a-zāīūṅñṭḍṇḷ])|\mbhikkhū' AND p.original !~* '\msati|satipaṭṭhān' ), shares AS ( SELECT translator, tok, count(*)::numeric/ sum(count(*)) OVER (PARTITION BY translator) AS p FROM g GROUP BY 1,2 ) SELECT translator, round(-sum(p*ln(p)),4) AS rendering_entropy_nats, count(*) AS distinct_tokens FROM shares GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY rendering_entropy_nats;

results.label: 10_dpd_ped_sense_ordering_baseline
results.sql: -- LEXICOGRAPHIC BASELINE: pull the DPD/PED/Monier dictionary entries for sati and its memory neighbors to record the sense-ORDER (does memory/recollection precede mindfulness in the headword gloss?). Descriptive anchor for the drift claim, not a test. SELECT source, headword, left(definition, 1200) AS def_excerpt FROM dictionary_entries WHERE source IN ('DPD','PED','Monier-Williams') AND headword ~* '^(sati|smṛti|anussati|paṭissati|apilāpana|asammosa|saraṇa)$' ORDER BY source, headword;
